creator: yasu@freebsd.org
text:
Created attachment 186008
patch file

After the commit of ports r449158, 'make install' fails as following:

root@maybe[1771]# pwd
/usr/ports/security/bruteforceblocker
root@maybe[1772]# make install
/!\ bruteforceblocker-1.2.4: Makefile warnings, please consider fixing /!\

Please set LICENSE for this port

===>   bruteforceblocker-1.2.4 depends on file: /usr/local/sbin/pkg - found
===> Fetching all distfiles required by bruteforceblocker-1.2.4 for building
===>  Extracting for bruteforceblocker-1.2.4
=> SHA256 Checksum OK for dgerzo-bruteforceblocker-1.2.4_GH0.tar.gz.
===>  Patching for bruteforceblocker-1.2.4
cd: /usr0/freebsd/ports/work/usr0/freebsd/ports/ports/security/bruteforceblocker/work/bruteforceblocker: No such file or directory
*** Error code 2

Stop.
make[1]: stopped in /usr0/freebsd/ports/ports/security/bruteforceblocker
*** Error code 1

Stop.
make: stopped in /usr0/freebsd/ports/ports/security/bruteforceblocker
root@maybe[1773]# 

attached patch fix it.

BTW, as is shown above there is no LICENSE setting in Makefile. I tried to fix it but found there is no description about license term of this software in its source code archive. So would you please consider adding license information in it?

Regards.
